Color Space Interpolation Tester

Recommended Color Space: OKLCH

Modern CSS allows us to generate new colors (like hover states or semantic scales) by mixing colors natively in the browser using color-mix().

  • sRGB (Standard): Often creates "muddy" or grayish dead zones in the middle when mixing opposing colors.
  • HSL (Hue/Saturation/Lightness): Better than sRGB, but perceptually uneven. A 50% lightness in blue looks much darker to the human eye than a 50% lightness in yellow.
  • OKLCH (Recommended): A perceptual color space. It guarantees that the mathematical middle step is exactly the visual middle step to the human eye. It maintains vibrant chromas and completely avoids the muddy grey dead zone.

1. Lightness Interpolation (Base to Light)

Testing how each space handles stepping from a dark/saturated base to its tinted light variant.

Navy
BASE
LIGHT
Teal
BASE
LIGHT
Gold
BASE
LIGHT
Orange
BASE
LIGHT
Red
BASE
LIGHT
Purple
BASE
LIGHT

2. Hue Interpolation (Base to Base)

Testing how the space handles blending entirely different hues. Notice how sRGB gets muddy, while OKLCH maintains vibrance.

Navy to Teal
NAVY
TEAL
Teal to Olive
TEAL
OLIVE
Olive to Gold
OLIVE
GOLD
Gold to Red
GOLD
RED
Red to Purple
RED
PURPLE
Extreme: Navy to Gold
NAVY
GOLD
